question: Has signal-to-structural conversion rate improved since S403 baseline (41.2%)?
expect: Signal audit reveals >=5 OPEN signals with implementing artifacts. Resolving these increases signal-to-structural rate from S403 baseline (41.2%).

method.approach: Re-measure signal-to-structural conversion rate for SIG-46..SIG-62 (n=17 signals, S420-S461 era). Compare to S403 measurement (41.2%, n=41 signals).

results.tool_artifact_rate: 29.4% (5/17 produced tool/code: SIG-48 open_lane.py, SIG-49 history_integrity.py, SIG-56 maintenance.py, SIG-59 question_gen.py, SIG-62 knowledge_recombine.py)
results.mechanism: Format change (L-660: session-section 89.7% vs table 39.0%) + periodic enforcement (S456: 60/60) + check.sh structural enforcement. L-601 confirmed.

actual: Signal-to-structural conversion improved 41.2% to 88.2% (+47pp). Tool conversion 29.4%. Full closure 76.5%. 0 OPEN signals (backlog structurally solved). Mechanism: format change (L-660) + periodic enforcement.
diff: Expected >=5 OPEN to resolve; actual 0 OPEN (S456 periodic already cleared). Measurement shifted to re-measurement. 88.2% vs 41.2% baseline = +47pp. Expectation direction CORRECT (improvement), magnitude EXCEEDED.
falsification: Conversion drops below 70% in next 20 signals, or OPEN backlog exceeds 10
